assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service A as HIGH risk and states it will completely block rolling updates since no pods can ever be unavailable

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service B as HIGH risk and explicitly states it has identical effect to 100% availability requirement

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service C as LOW risk and explains it allows rolling updates while maintaining 2/3 pods available

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service D as LOW risk and states it allows 1 pod to be unavailable while maintaining 4/5 pods during upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Provides specific YAML configuration recommending minAvailable: 80% or maxUnavailable: 20% for Service A

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Provides specific YAML configuration recommending maxUnavailable: 1 for Service B

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE-specific behavior or the 1-hour timeout for PDB respect during surge upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: Provides complete YAML manifests but does not include kubectl patch commands for making the changes

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: Mentions testing with kubectl drain dry-run but does not specifically recommend testing PDB changes in non-production environments first
